summary:
EXECUTIVE READ
India activity concentrates in Colo (281 signals) and Build (18), led by Tamil Nadu, Delhi, Maharashtra, Gujarat, and Telangana. The top India policy hook is the Notice of Attachment of Bank Accounts and Demat Accounts (three filings in June 2026). Value-chain layers moving on India are Colo operators (Sify, NTT India, AdaniConneX) and Build players (NTT India). Top movers this run: Sify (score 77.5, Δ0.0, 1 new signal), NTT India (score 35.4, Δ0.0, 1 new signal), AdaniConneX (score 30.6, Δ0.0, 1 new signal).

Analysis: Sify is actively expanding its Colo footprint in Tamil Nadu with a 25-acre land acquisition for a ₹15,266-crore data centre project. Its recent 6-K filings highlight alignment with the Union Budget’s tax holiday for foreign cloud players utilizing Indian infrastructure, signaling intent to scale hyperscale investments. The combination of land acquisition, policy-responsive filings, and facility presence indicates a strategic push to capture growing demand from foreign cloud players entering India.
